title: A little life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
review: This book gives me goosebumps every time I talk of it. Gives a new meaning to 'it's not your fault' and emotional trauma. It's devastating, painful, depressing, and beautiful. It's also too much.

title: Abundance
author: Ezra Klein, Derek Thompson
review: It's a stunning analysis of everything that's wrong with US bureaucracy and inefficiency. But you're left with a feeling of "well yeah, obviously; but who wouldn't want that?!" Or, as Bernie put it, where is the ideology?

title: Philosophical Investigations
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
review: Wittgenstein disrupts all of philosophy, and does it best in this book. It will only mean something to you if you've read everything that came before it; but it will mean a lot to you when you have.

title: La superba
author: Ilya Leonard Pfeijffer
review: Made me have to go to genua. It's an ode to the city, italian culture, hilariously funny, and full of perversion.

title: If anyone builds it everyone dies: Why superhuman AI would kill us all
spineTitle: If anyone builds it everyone dies
author: Eliezer Yudkowski & Nate Soares
review: A convincing read on why modern AI systems should be regulated, what all the dangers are, and why we shouldn't want to build it if we know what's good for us. I agree, except that the G-AI race is already destroying societies, which should be a much more compelling; and it isn't or doesn't seem to be.
